title: cornus oligophlebia
intro: Cornus oligophlebia, also known as the Himalayan dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cornus oligophlebia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Cornus oligophlebia is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
